input.initialPrompt: Analyze this project for incremental build issues. Identify any custom targets that would unnecessarily re-run on every build even when nothing has changed.

data.reasoningText: The user wants me to analyze the project for incremental build issues. Let me look at the project files to understand the build configuration.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>
25.

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6)
- **Problem:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild always runs it. Worse, it writes `$([System.DateTime]::Now)` — a volatile value that changes every build — into `build-timestamp.txt`, causing downstream targets to detect a changed file and rebuild.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13)
- **Problem:** Also missing `Inputs`/`Outputs`, so it always re-executes. It runs `git rev-parse HEAD` and writes `git-hash.g.cs` every time. Even if the git hash hasn't changed, the file is **overwritten unconditionally** (`Overwrite="true"`), updating its timestamp and triggering recompilation.

### Why This Breaks No-Op Builds

Both targets run `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile"` with no skip conditions. On every build:
1. `build-timestamp.txt` gets a new timestamp → always different content.
2. `git-hash.g.cs` is rewritten → file timestamp changes even if content is the same.

This means **CoreCompile will never be skipped**, even when source code hasn't changed.

### Fixes

- **GenerateTimestamp:** Remove it entirely, or gate it with `Inputs`/`Outputs` and write a deterministic value (e.g., a commit-based version) so the file only changes when inputs actually change.
- **EmbedGitHash:** Add `Inputs="$(ProjectDir).git/HEAD;$(ProjectDir).git/refs/heads/**"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"` so MSBuild skips the target when the hash hasn't changed. Additionally, use a mechanism that only writes the file when content differs (e.g., compare before writing).
